key_insight: For echocardiography segmentation with partially labelled data, the best loss function depends on the missing-label pattern: aBCE and marginal loss work well when one label is missing, while marginal loss is strongest when multiple labels are missing.
clinical_relevance: Choosing the right loss function could improve the reliability of automated heart-structure segmentation across hospitals and datasets, supporting more consistent cardiac biomarker measurement.
method: Compared adaptive categorical cross entropy (aCCE), marginal loss, and adaptive binary cross entropy (aBCE).
Tested intra-domain vs inter-domain segmentation settings with single and multiple missing labels.
Evaluated different proportions of fully labelled and partially labelled training data across network architectures.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a comprehensive comparison across multiple scenarios and finds all three losses perform well in intra-domain tasks, with marginal loss generally leading in harder inter-domain, multi-missing-label cases.
watch_item: Results are specific to echocardiography segmentation setups, so performance may not transfer directly to other imaging tasks, label structures, or annotation protocols.
paper_title: Comparison of Loss Functions for Robust Deep Learning-based Echocardiography Segmentation when Learning with Partially Labelled Data from Multiple Domains
